title: [phase:critique] [family:reviewer-lane] [provider_hint:codex-sidecar] Review the auth-hardening remaining-task changes only. Files: /Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts, /Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts, /Users/masayuki/...
summary_text: 1. **P1: Tenant fallback can grant unintended cross-tenant access on tenant lookup miss/failure.** `authenticateAndAuthorize` always resolves a tenant, and `resolveTenantId` falls back to `'default'` when tenant mapping is missing or lookup fails, instead of denying access. That can authorize an unmapped user into shared default-tenant data. Refs: [cockpit-api.ts#L279](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts#L279), [cockpit-api.ts#L325](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts#L325), [cockpit-api.ts#L332](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts#L332) 2. **P1: Refresh flow can consume/rotate token, then return misleading `404` on transient auth-state lookup failure.** `handleRefreshToken` rotates first, then does a second auth-state lookup via `getUserAuthState`. That helper collapses lookup failures to `null`, so DB outages/regressions are reported as “User not found” (`404`). This can strand the client after token rotation with a non-retryable-looking error. Refs: [cockpit-api.ts#L522](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts#L522), [cockpit-api.ts#L530](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts#L530), [cockpit-api.ts#L533](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts#L533), [jwt-auth.ts#L461](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts#L461), [jwt-auth.ts#L466](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts#L466) 3. **P2: Related tests cover only happy path for change-password; key negative/security cases are untested.** Current test file has only one success-case test. Missing cases include wrong current password (`401` + no mutation), access-only user without password hash (`400`), and refresh endpoint behavior when post-rotation auth-state lookup fails (should distinguish `503` vs `404`). Refs: [cockpit-api.auth.test.ts#L87](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.auth.test.ts#L87), [jwt-auth.test.ts#L139](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.test.ts#L139) Could not execute Vitest in this sandbox due EPERM on temp/config timestamp file creation, so findings are from static review only.
